main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Evaluating Then Aligning (ETA) framework","Multimodal evaluation (pregeneration evaluation of visual inputs and post-generation evaluation of textual outputs)","Bi-level alignment (shallow alignment using interference prefix, deep alignment using sentence-level best-of-N search)"],"problems":["Safety vulnerabilities of Vision Language Models (VLMs) to adversarial visual inputs","Resource-intensiveness and limitations of existing defense methods (fine-tuning and inference-based)","Inability of current methods to ensure both safety and usefulness in VLM responses","Lack of robust multimodal safety evaluation (especially for visual inputs)"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Unsafe rate (in cross-modality attacks)","Helpfulness (GPT-4 win-tie rate)","Harmlessness","Efficiency"],"results":[{"metric":"Unsafe rate reduction (cross-modality attacks)","value":"87.5%"},{"metric":"GPT-4 helpfulness win-tie rate","value":"96.6%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes ETA, a novel inference-time VLM alignment framework requiring no additional data or training (plug-and-play).","Decomposes defense into two phases: multimodal evaluation and bi-level alignment, ensuring both safety and usefulness without compromising general capabilities.","Introduces a multimodal evaluator that assesses both visual and textual inputs/outputs for robust safety awareness.","Presents a bi-level alignment strategy: shallow alignment via interference prefix and deep alignment via sentence-level best-of-N search.","Provides new insights into the failure of existing VLM defense mechanisms due to the continuous nature of visual token embeddings."]}

methods: Evaluating Then Aligning (ETA) framework
Multimodal evaluation (pregeneration evaluation of visual inputs and post-generation evaluation of textual outputs)
Bi-level alignment (shallow alignment using interference prefix, deep alignment using sentence-level best-of-N search)
problems: Safety vulnerabilities of Vision Language Models (VLMs) to adversarial visual inputs
Resource-intensiveness and limitations of existing defense methods (fine-tuning and inference-based)
Inability of current methods to ensure both safety and usefulness in VLM responses
Lack of robust multimodal safety evaluation (especially for visual inputs)
novelty_claims: Proposes ETA, a novel inference-time VLM alignment framework requiring no additional data or training (plug-and-play).
Decomposes defense into two phases: multimodal evaluation and bi-level alignment, ensuring both safety and usefulness without compromising general capabilities.
Introduces a multimodal evaluator that assesses both visual and textual inputs/outputs for robust safety awareness.
Presents a bi-level alignment strategy: shallow alignment via interference prefix and deep alignment via sentence-level best-of-N search.
Provides new insights into the failure of existing VLM defense mechanisms due to the continuous nature of visual token embeddings.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Bi-Modal Adversarial Prompt Attack (BAP): jointly optimizing both visual and textual prompts to jailbreak LVLMs","Embedding universal adversarial perturbations in images using a few-shot query-agnostic corpus","Optimizing textual prompts with specific harmful intent using LLM-based chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning and feedback iteration"],"problems":["Existing jailbreak attacks on LVLMs focus only on the visual modality and are ineffective against models that fuse visual and textual features","Need for more effective red-teaming/jailbreak strategies that can bypass safety guardrails in LVLMs by attacking both modalities"],"datasets":["SafetyBench","AdvBench"],"metrics":["Attack Success Rate"],"results":[{"metric":"Attack Success Rate (average improvement over baselines)","value":"+29.03%"}],"novelty_claims":["First to propose a bi-modal (visual and textual) adversarial prompt attack (BAP) for jailbreaking LVLMs, overcoming the limitations of single-modality attacks","Introduces a two-stage attack: universal adversarial image perturbation followed by intent-specific textual prompt optimization using LLMs and chain-of-thought reasoning","Demonstrates effectiveness on both open-source and commercial LVLMs, including black-box models","Shows potential for universal attacks to generate biased outputs and evaluate model robustness"]}

methods: Bi-Modal Adversarial Prompt Attack (BAP): jointly optimizing both visual and textual prompts to jailbreak LVLMs
Embedding universal adversarial perturbations in images using a few-shot query-agnostic corpus
Optimizing textual prompts with specific harmful intent using LLM-based chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning and feedback iteration
problems: Existing jailbreak attacks on LVLMs focus only on the visual modality and are ineffective against models that fuse visual and textual features
Need for more effective red-teaming/jailbreak strategies that can bypass safety guardrails in LVLMs by attacking both modalities
datasets: SafetyBench
AdvBench

results.metric: Attack Success Rate (average improvement over baselines)

novelty_claims: First to propose a bi-modal (visual and textual) adversarial prompt attack (BAP) for jailbreaking LVLMs, overcoming the limitations of single-modality attacks
Introduces a two-stage attack: universal adversarial image perturbation followed by intent-specific textual prompt optimization using LLMs and chain-of-thought reasoning
Demonstrates effectiveness on both open-source and commercial LVLMs, including black-box models
Shows potential for universal attacks to generate biased outputs and evaluate model robustness

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Adaptive Shield Prompting (AdaShield): prepending defense prompts to model inputs to defend against structure-based jailbreak attacks","AdaShield-Static (AdaShield-S): manually designed static defense prompt for MLLMs","AdaShield-Adaptive (AdaShield-A): adaptive auto-refinement framework using a target MLLM and a Defender LLM to iteratively optimize and generate a diverse pool of defense prompts"],"problems":["Vulnerability of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to structure-based jailbreak attacks, where harmful semantic content is injected into images to bypass safety mechanisms","Lack of effective defenses against structure-based jailbreak attacks, especially for black-box MLLMs or MLLM-as-a-Service (MLMaaS) settings where fine-tuning is infeasible"],"datasets":["FigStep (structure-based jailbreak attack dataset)","QR (structure-based jailbreak attack dataset)","Standard benign datasets for evaluating general capabilities (exact names not specified in the provided text)"],"metrics":["Robustness against structure-based jailbreak attacks (attack success rate)","Performance on benign tasks (accuracy or other standard metrics, though not specified in the provided text)"],"results":[{"metric":"Defense success rate against structure-based jailbreak attacks (AdaShield-A vs. baselines)","value":"AdaShield-A achieves superior defense performance compared to baselines (exact numbers not provided in the text)"},{"metric":"Performance on benign tasks","value":"No significant degradation; model's general capabilities are maintained (exact numbers not provided in the text)"}],"novelty_claims":["AdaShield is the first framework to defend MLLMs against structure-based jailbreak attacks by prepending adaptive defense prompts, without requiring fine-tuning or training additional modules.","AdaShield works in black-box settings and is applicable to MLLM-as-a-Service (MLMaaS) scenarios, where model parameters and gradients are inaccessible.","The adaptive auto-refinement framework (AdaShield-A) iteratively optimizes defense prompts using a Defender LLM and a target MLLM, generating a diverse pool of prompts tailored to various attack scenarios.","AdaShield achieves strong robustness against structure-based jailbreak attacks while preserving the model's performance on benign tasks."]}

methods: Adaptive Shield Prompting (AdaShield): prepending defense prompts to model inputs to defend against structure-based jailbreak attacks
AdaShield-Static (AdaShield-S): manually designed static defense prompt for MLLMs
AdaShield-Adaptive (AdaShield-A): adaptive auto-refinement framework using a target MLLM and a Defender LLM to iteratively optimize and generate a diverse pool of defense prompts
problems: Vulnerability of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to structure-based jailbreak attacks, where harmful semantic content is injected into images to bypass safety mechanisms
Lack of effective defenses against structure-based jailbreak attacks, especially for black-box MLLMs or MLLM-as-a-Service (MLMaaS) settings where fine-tuning is infeasible
datasets: FigStep (structure-based jailbreak attack dataset)
QR (structure-based jailbreak attack dataset)
Standard benign datasets for evaluating general capabilities (exact names not specified in the provided text)
metrics: Robustness against structure-based jailbreak attacks (attack success rate)
Performance on benign tasks (accuracy or other standard metrics, though not specified in the provided text)

results.metric: Defense success rate against structure-based jailbreak attacks (AdaShield-A vs. baselines)
results.value: AdaShield-A achieves superior defense performance compared to baselines (exact numbers not provided in the text)
No significant degradation; model's general capabilities are maintained (exact numbers not provided in the text)

novelty_claims: AdaShield is the first framework to defend MLLMs against structure-based jailbreak attacks by prepending adaptive defense prompts, without requiring fine-tuning or training additional modules.
AdaShield works in black-box settings and is applicable to MLLM-as-a-Service (MLMaaS) scenarios, where model parameters and gradients are inaccessible.

The adaptive auto-refinement framework (AdaShield-A) iteratively optimizes defense prompts using a Defender LLM and a target MLLM, generating a diverse pool of prompts tailored to various attack scenarios.
AdaShield achieves strong robustness against structure-based jailbreak attacks while preserving the model's performance on benign tasks.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ECSO (Eyes Closed, Safety On): a training-free protection strategy for MLLMs that detects unsafe responses and, if detected, transforms image inputs into text via a query-aware image-to-text (I2T) transformation, then generates responses using the text-only LLM mode to restore safety mechanisms"],"problems":["Vulnerability of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to jailbreak attacks via image inputs","Suppression of pre-aligned LLM safety mechanisms in MLLMs when images are introduced","Difficulty in transferring LLM safety mechanisms to MLLMs, especially without retraining or human intervention"],"datasets":["MM-SafetyBench (SD+OCR)","VLSafe","Five state-of-the-art MLLM benchmarks (exact names not all specified in the excerpt)"],"metrics":["Safety improvement (percentage)","Utility performance on MLLM benchmarks"],"results":[{"metric":"Safety improvement on MM-SafetyBench (SD+OCR) with LLaVA-1.5-7B","value":"37.6%"},{"metric":"Safety improvement on VLSafe with LLaVA-1.5-7B","value":"71.3%"}],"novelty_claims":["ECSO is a novel, training-free, and self-contained protection strategy for MLLMs that does not require additional supervised finetuning or human intervention.","ECSO leverages the intrinsic safety mechanisms of pre-aligned LLMs by adaptively transforming unsafe image inputs into text, restoring the LLM's safety mechanism.","ECSO can serve as a data engine to generate supervised-finetuning (SFT) data for MLLM alignment without extra human intervention."]}

methods: ECSO (Eyes Closed, Safety On): a training-free protection strategy for MLLMs that detects unsafe responses and, if detected, transforms image inputs into text via a query-aware image-to-text (I2T) transformation, then generates responses using the text-only LLM mode to restore safety mechanisms
problems: Vulnerability of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to jailbreak attacks via image inputs
Suppression of pre-aligned LLM safety mechanisms in MLLMs when images are introduced
Difficulty in transferring LLM safety mechanisms to MLLMs, especially without retraining or human intervention
datasets: MM-SafetyBench (SD+OCR)
VLSafe
Five state-of-the-art MLLM benchmarks (exact names not all specified in the excerpt)

results.metric: Safety improvement on MM-SafetyBench (SD+OCR) with LLaVA-1.5-7B
Safety improvement on VLSafe with LLaVA-1.5-7B

novelty_claims: ECSO is a novel, training-free, and self-contained protection strategy for MLLMs that does not require additional supervised finetuning or human intervention.
ECSO leverages the intrinsic safety mechanisms of pre-aligned LLMs by adaptively transforming unsafe image inputs into text, restoring the LLM's safety mechanism.
ECSO can serve as a data engine to generate supervised-finetuning (SFT) data for MLLM alignment without extra human intervention.

methods: MLLMGuard: a multi-dimensional safety evaluation suite for MLLMs, including a bilingual (English and Chinese) image-text evaluation dataset, inference utilities, and a lightweight evaluator

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["MM-SafetyBench: a comprehensive framework for safety-critical evaluation of MLLMs against image-based manipulations","Visual prompt attack: generating query-relevant images using text-to-image generation (Stable Diffusion) and typography based on extracted keywords from malicious queries","Blending two types of query-relevant images to provoke inappropriate responses from MLLMs","Safety prompting: introducing prompts that instruct the model to reject responding to malicious questions"],"problems":["Lack of systematic safety evaluation for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs)","Vulnerability of MLLMs to attacks using query-relevant images that bypass safety mechanisms","Insufficient robustness of current MLLMs against multimodal (image+text) adversarial attacks"],"datasets":["MM-SafetyBench: a dataset of 5,040 text-image pairs across 13 safety-critical scenarios"],"metrics":["Attack success rate"],"results":[{"metric":"Attack success rate (using query-relevant images)","value":"Much higher than using query-irrelevant images; specific values not provided in the excerpt"},{"metric":"Attack success rate (after introducing safety prompts)","value":"Significant decrease; specific values not provided in the excerpt"}],"novelty_claims":["First comprehensive safety evaluation benchmark (MM-SafetyBench) specifically for MLLMs using multimodal (image+text) attacks","Novel visual prompt attack method that uses query-relevant images generated via Stable Diffusion and typography to bypass MLLM safety mechanisms","Demonstration that even safety-aligned MLLMs are vulnerable to these attacks","Proposal and validation of a simple prompting strategy (safety prompts) to enhance MLLM resilience against such attacks"]}

novelty_claims: Proposes a fully automatic method for generating adversarial suffixes that reliably induce objectionable content in aligned LLMs, surpassing previous manual and automatic approaches.
Demonstrates high transferability of adversarial suffixes across multiple prompts and models, including black-box, publicly released LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT, Bard, Claude).

Establishes new state-of-the-art in adversarial attacks against aligned LLMs, with significantly higher success rates than previous methods.
